attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who wins from reduced groups on steep summit finishes (18%+ gradients). Excels when strong break gets freedom from GC teams, typically finishing climbs rather than initiating long-range solo moves.
team_tactics: Limited domestique support evident - crashes exposed inadequate team protection multiple times. Functions as sacrificial domestique for teammates (HIRT work at UAE Tour) rather than receiving lead-out or positioning support.
terrain_preference: Thrives on exceptionally steep gradients (18%+) in summit finishes where pure climbing power dominates. Best suited to reduced breakaway groups (8 riders) on single defining climb. Exposed on technical descents and nervous flat finales.
vulnerability: Significant bike handling issues in bunch scenarios - crashed on 4 separate documented occasions including consecutive days in leader's jersey. Vulnerable in nervous finales and fast straight roads despite correct positioning attempts.
key_patterns: Breakaway specialist: won stage and GC lead from 8-rider break, documented across multiple races
Crashes frequently (4 instances): consecutive days in red jersey, inadequate positioning in finales, handling deficit
Exceptional steep gradient power: dominated climbers on 18% pitches at age 34, Picon Blanco winner
Veteran domestique role: willingly sacrifices personal GC for team tempo work (UAE Tour evidence)
Late-climb resilience: picked back up on final climbs after difficulties, third-place finishes
form_trajectory: negative: Despite 2022 climbing resilience (third place finish), repeated crashes and shift to pure domestique role suggest declining status from 2021 stage winner/GC contender to support rider
